notes: v1.14.37 — globe testing build: open facing North America + auto-warp DISABLED behind a reversible flag. Four surgical edits in the landing globe IIFE, no markup/CSS. (1) After AUS_ROT is computed, seed rotY = AUS_ROT so the globe opens Austin-centered with the whole NA hub cluster in frame, then the existing auto-spin drifts off on its own (interacted starts false → auto-spin branch, not magnetic-lock; under prefers-reduced-motion it holds on Austin). (2) New flag var ENTER_ENABLED = false. (3) Auto-warp trigger gated: if (ENTER_ENABLED && online >= 0.995) startWarp(). (4) Keyboard enter path gated: Enter/Space only warps if ENTER_ENABLED. Net effect: the globe stays up for on-device inspection — spin freely, let Austin lock dead-center, it holds and does NOT enter the app. The no-WebGL tap-to-enter fallback (GL-absent only, not reached on iPhone Safari) left untouched. To restore entry, flip ENTER_ENABLED = true (that's the next ship, v1.14.38). node --check 3/3 inline + sw.js PASS, four stamps lockstep v1.14.37, cache bumped (so the iPhone pulls it), Block-0 CSS untouched (no brace re-count). DEVICE-CHECK (John, iPhone): globe opens centered on NA (Austin + cluster), starts rotating on its own; dragging Austin to center locks-and-holds, never enters.
